title: Refactor a guard-using-forbidden-function into a guard-and-body split
prompt: Given: `def valid_email?(email) when is_binary(email) and String.contains?(email, "@"), do: true; def valid_email?(_), do: false`. This is a compile error — `String.contains?/2` is not allowed in guards. Split: keep `when is_binary(email)` as the guard, move the `@` check to the body, and add a catch-all clause. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.EmailCheck do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the restricted-subset rule for guards. Sonnet often writes this exact anti-pattern.
